title: Technical Abstract
generation_instructions: Write a technical abstract that summarizes the project's research goals, methods, and expected outcomes. Include specific details about the research plan, key experiments, and anticipated results, emphasizing the technical aspects. Ensure that the abstract clearly states the scientific problem being addressed and the proposed approach.

title: General Audience Abstract
generation_instructions: Compose a general audience abstract that explains the project's goals and potential impact in a way that is accessible to non-scientists. Describe the relevance of the research to melanoma prevention, detection, diagnosis, or treatment. Focus on the potential benefits of the research for patients and public health.

title: Project Description
generation_instructions: Develop a comprehensive project description, including the background, specific aims, preliminary data, experimental design, methods, statistical plan, figures, and rationale, that will be broken into subsections. Ensure that the project description covers all the necessary information for the grant application, addressing all aspects of the proposed research. Describe the key aims of the project, and detail how each aim will be addressed by the experimental design and analysis strategy.

title: Background and specific aims
generation_instructions: Provide the necessary background information and state the specific aims of the project. Start with a brief introduction to the relevant background literature, and clearly define the knowledge gap this proposal addresses. Detail the specific aims that will accomplish the objective of the proposal. The specific aims should build on the background and clearly state what the project intends to achieve, and should be written in an easily actionable way.

title: Preliminary data
generation_instructions: Present the preliminary data that supports the feasibility of the proposed research. Include previously generated data that directly supports the project goals and methods. Highlight any prior findings that establish the basis for the current project's goals. Explain how the data supports the feasibility of the study design.

title: Experimental design and methods
generation_instructions: Describe the experimental design and methods, including all research protocols, materials, and procedures. Explain in detail how experiments will be conducted, and how data will be collected. Ensure that each experimental step is clearly outlined with necessary detail.

title: Statistical plan
generation_instructions: Provide a detailed explanation of the statistical plan for data analysis. Describe the methods for data analysis, sample size calculations and power analyses. Explain how the statistical methods will be used to draw conclusions from the data and ensure the study's rigor and validity. Discuss statistical approaches appropriate for each experimental design, and how the proposed data will be managed and analyzed.

title: Figures
generation_instructions: Provide a brief description of the figures that are included with the project description, and explain their purpose and what they convey. This section is a summary of the figures.

title: Rationale/fit with key criteria, including the potential for clinical impact
generation_instructions: Explain the rationale for the project and how it fits with the key criteria outlined in the call for proposals. Emphasize the potential for clinical impact, and describe the translational potential of the research. Articulate the relevance of the project, and its ability to address current challenges in melanoma research and treatment.
